SELECT value, isnan(value) AS is_nan FROM test_table;
SELECT AVG(value) AS avg_value, SUM(isnan(value)) AS nan_count FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE additional_table (id INT, float_value FLOAT);
SELECT a.id, a.float_value, b.value, isnan(b.value) AS is_nan FROM additional_table a JOIN test_table b ON a.float_value = b.value;
SELECT isnan((SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table)) AS is_max_nan;
SELECT isnan(AVG(value)) AS is_avg_nan FROM test_table;
SELECT (CASE WHEN isnan(value) THEN 'Not a Number' ELSE 'Valid Number' END) AS value_status FROM test_table;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE isnan(value);
